data.title: MarineMax Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.01, revenue of $527.4M misses by $88.35M
data.description:
• MarineMax press release (HZO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $527.4M (-16.5% Y/Y) misses by $88.35M.
  • This decline, primarily driven by lower boat sales, was partially offset by continued growth in higher-margin businesses, including finance and insurance, superyacht services and marinas.
  • Same-store sales decreased 15% due to challenging environment, compared to an increase of 11% in the prior-year period
  • Based on current business conditions, retail marine industry trends, and other relevant factors, the Company continues to expect fiscal 2026 Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $110 million to $125 million and adjusted net income in the range of $0.40 to $0.95 per diluted share vs. $0.74 consensus

data.title: FirstCash Non-GAAP EPS of $2.69 beats by $0.30, revenue of $1.05B beats by $40M
data.description:
• FirstCash press release (FCFS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.69 beats by $0.30.
  • Revenue of $1.05B (+25.5% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • The company also announced that the Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.42 per share, which will be paid in May 2026.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the first quarter increased 29% to $211 million compared to the prior-year quarter.

  • 2026 Outlook

    The outlook for the remainder of 2026 continues to be highly positive. The company is raising its expectations for year-over-year growth in pawn segment revenues, driven by the continued growth in same-store pawn receivables and better than anticipated revenue contributions from stores acquired in 2025.

data.title: IBM slides on unchanged outlook, ranks as most overvalued among IT consulting firms
data.description:
IBM (IBM) shares tumbled 7% despite beating first-quarter estimates on nearly every metric, a sharp market verdict on management's decision to leave its full-year guidance unchanged even after posting adjusted EPS of $1.91, revenue of $15.92 billion, and free cash flow of $2.22 billion, all comfortably above Wall Street's expectations.

Despite being the largest company on the list, IBM ranks at the bottom of this valuation ranking among IT consulting and other services stocks above $2B market cap. It carries only a C+ valuation grade, trailing peers such as DXC Technology (DXC) and Kyndryl (KD), which both earn A+ grades, as well as Amdocs (DOX) and EPAM Systems (EPAM), which hold A- grades. 

In other words, investors may still view IBM as fundamentally solid, but not especially cheap relative to its growth outlook and sector peers.

Against that backdrop, the following list ranks IT consulting and other services stocks by valuation grade, which measures how expensive or inexpensive a stock appears relative to its fundamentals. An A+ grade signals the most undervalued names, while lower grades indicate stocks that trade at richer multiples.

Here is the list:

  1. DXC Technology Company (DXC), Valuation grade: A+

  2. Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (KD), Valuation grade: A+

  3. Amdocs Limited (DOX), Valuation grade: A-

  4. EPAM Systems, Inc. (EPAM), Valuation grade: A-

  5. Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (CTSH), Valuation grade: B+

  6. Gartner, Inc. (IT), Valuation grade: B+

  7. International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), Valuation grade: C+

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Mobileye to buy back $250M of shares
data.description:
Mobileye (MBLY) on Thursday said its board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $250M of the company’s outstanding Class A common stock.

The buyback is intended to partially offset dilution due to share-based compensation and shares issued in connection with the Mentee Robotics acquisition, while maintaining flexibility to invest in its core automotive business and Physical AI platform, Mobileye said in a statement. 

Repurchases may be made from time to time through open-market purchases, privately negotiated deals, or accelerated share repurchase agreements, subject to market conditions and other factors.

“Our primary capital allocation priority remains investing in R&D and capital expenditures to support innovation and execute on our expanding product roadmap," said Mobileye CEO.

Mobileye said it does not intend to directly repurchase shares of its Class A or Class B common stock for cash from Intel or other related parties, under the buyback program. 

Intel (INTC) owns a stake in the Israeli company that develops autonomous driving technology and advanced driver assistance systems. 

Mobileye said it expects to fund the repurchase program through existing cash on hand and future cash flows.Shares up +2.91% in premarket trading. The company is also reporting its Q1 earnings on Thursday.

data.title: Apogee declares $0.27 dividend
data.description:
• Apogee (APOG) declares $0.27/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.04%
  • Payable May 28; for shareholders of record May 13; ex-div May 13.
  • See APOG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Snap-On GAAP EPS of $4.69 misses by $0.06, revenue of $1.21B beats by $30M; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Snap-On press release (SNA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $4.69 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $1.21B (+6.1% Y/Y) beats by $30M.

  • Gross margin of 50.4%, including 40 basis points of unfavorable currency effects, compares to 50.7% last year;

    Tools segment sales up 5.0% from a year ago, organic sales up 3.4%

  • Projects that capital expenditures in 2026 will approximate $100 million, of which $21.2 million was incurred in the first three months of the year.

  • Snap-on currently anticipates that its full-year 2026 effective income tax rate will be in the range of 22% to 23%.

data.title: PulteGroup boosts stock repurchase plan by $1.5B
data.description:
• PulteGroup (PHM) board has approved a $1.5B increase to the company’s share repurchase authorization.
  • This increase brings the company’s current share repurchase authorization to $2.1B.
  • “PulteGroup continues to allocate capital in alignment with its stated priorities of investing in the business, paying dividends, and returning excess funds to shareholders,” said Ryan Marshall, PulteGroup President and CEO. “Over the past 10 years, we have invested $38 billion into our business while returning $9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PulteGroup GAAP EPS of $1.79 misses by $0.02, revenue of $3.41B beats by $30M
data.description:
• PulteGroup press release (PHM): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.79 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $3.41B (-12.3% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Net New Orders Increased 3% to 8,034 Homes with a Value of $4.6 Billion
  • Closed 6,102 Homes Generating Home Sale Revenues of $3.3 Billion
  • Home Sale Gross Margin of 24.4%
  • Unit Backlog of 10,427 Homes with a Value of $6.5 Billion
  • Shares +1% PM.

data.title: Lithium Ion Energy announces name change to Osiris One Metals
data.description:
• Lithium Ion Energy (ION:CA) has approved the company's corporate name change from Lithium Ion Energy to Osiris One Metals
  • Effective at the opening of trading on Monday, April 27, 2026, the common shares of the Company will commence trading on the TSXV under the new name Osiris One Metals Ltd. and the new trading symbol "OSM". 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dow in charts: Revenue slides broadly Y/Y, except in Latin America and Performace Materials
